faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Quick answers about NYT Connections and how our hints work.

faqItems.question: What is NYT Connections?
faqItems.answer: NYT Connections is a daily word puzzle by The New York Times. You are given 16 words and must sort them into four groups of four, each sharing a hidden connection. The groups are color-coded by difficulty: yellow (easiest), green, blue, and purple (hardest).

faqItems.question: How do the progressive hints work?
faqItems.answer: Each group has three hint levels. Level 1 gives a vague thematic clue. Level 2 narrows it down with a more specific hint. Level 3 reveals the category name. You choose how much help you want.

faqItems.question: When does the daily Connections puzzle reset?
faqItems.answer: A new NYT Connections puzzle is released every day at midnight Eastern Time (ET). Our hints are published shortly after.

faqItems.question: Is this site affiliated with The New York Times?
faqItems.answer: No. ConnectionsHint is an independent fan site. We provide hints and discussion to help players enjoy the puzzle. NYT Connections is a trademark of The New York Times Company.

faqItems.question: Can I browse past puzzle answers?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Our archive page has every past puzzle organized by date, with full answers, group themes, and difficulty ratings.

faqItems.question: What is the best strategy for solving Connections?
faqItems.answer: Start by looking for the easiest group (yellow). Scan all 16 words for obvious themes first. Beware of words that could fit multiple groups — the purple group often uses tricky wordplay or less obvious connections.
